title: Gregg Rickman and Others on Philip K. Dick (Review)
author: Istvan Csicsery-Ronay, Jr.
card_summary: Review essay by Istvan Csicsery-Ronay, Jr. in Science Fiction Studies (Vol. 22, No. 3, November 1995) reviewing Samuel J. Umland's "Philip K. Dick: Contemporary Critical Interpretations" alongside Gregg Rickman's contributions to Dick scholarship.
page_summary: Extended review essay by Istvan Csicsery-Ronay, Jr. in Science Fiction Studies (Vol. 22, No. 3, November 1995) reviewing Samuel J. Umland's anthology "Philip K. Dick: Contemporary Critical Interpretations" (Greenwood Press, 1995). The review surveys ten previously unpublished articles and two reprints covering various aspects of Dick's work from different critical perspectives. Csicsery-Ronay notes the absence of feminist criticism and queer theory, while evaluating contributions from Gregg Rickman and others that fill gaps left by earlier Dick scholarship. The essay provides a comprehensive overview of the state of Dick studies in the mid-1990s.
